attacking_style: Primarily a position-holder rather than explosive attacker; stays present in key selections on climbs but lacks final Monument-winning punch on decisive terrain.
team_tactics: Functions as high-level domestique on GT mountain stages when healthy, sacrificing own position to support GC leaders (notably Aru in 2017).
terrain_preference: One-day Monument terrain (evidenced by Il Lombardia 4th) suits better than Grand Tour mountains where injury vulnerability and team duties limit impact.
vulnerability: Catastrophic crash susceptibility at Tour de France specifically—crashed in 3 of 3 observed Tours, including race-ending abandon while top-10 GC contender.
key_patterns: Tour de France crash pattern: injured in every observed appearance (2017 arm, 2019 facial/abandon).
Lacks winning acceleration in Monument finals despite consistent selection presence (Il Lombardia 4th).
Injury timing clusters before/during mountain stages, eliminating GC contention or domestique effectiveness.
Performs stronger in one-day races than multi-week GTs when crash-free.
form_trajectory: neutral: 2019 Lombardia 4th showed peak season form, but Tour abandon from crash (not fitness) prevents clear trajectory assessment
